content.claim: Hungary has one of the highest levels of socio-economic segregation between secondary schools in the OECD, with school selection based on student records creating strong stratification effects comparable to Latin American systems
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Direction of causality is contested: unclear whether tracking drives segregation or interacts with residential sorting and free school choice
Analysis is correlational rather than causal
Magnitude of selection effects disputed among researchers
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
